title: user-level (define (raise x) ...) cannot shadow builtin raise — calls hit the R7RS exception builtin
goal: Found by the SICP ch2 corpus (PR #106). A top-level user definition of `raise` does not shadow the builtin R7RS exception raise: calls to the user function dispatch to the builtin and abort with 'Unhandled exception: user exception'. R7RS allows user bindings to shadow any identifier including builtins; the shadowable-op machinery exists (cf. shadowable_op_letrec_test.esk, the `walk` redirection fix in #89) but does not cover `raise` (and possibly other exception/control builtins: error, raise-continuable, with-exception-handler?).
minimal_repro: (define (raise x) (list 'raised x)) (display (raise 5))  ;; aborts: Unhandled exception: user exception — must print (raised 5)
acceptance: the repro prints (raised 5); builtin raise unaffected when not shadowed; audit error/raise-continuable/with-exception-handler for the same gap; -r AND AOT; shadowable_op tests still pass
campaign_payoff: R7RS conformance: user code may rebind any identifier. Unblocks book-faithful SICP code that names tower functions raise.
